data.name: Validate_WarnBelowCrit
data.value: The warning limit must be lower than the critical limit

data.name: Threshold_FanStopped
data.value: Fan {0} has stopped (0 RPM) while the CPU is at {1:0} °C

data.name: WinEvent_KernelPower
data.value: The computer shut down unexpectedly or crashed (Kernel-Power 41)

data.name: WinEvent_Bsod
data.value: Windows crashed with a blue screen (BSOD, BugCheck 1001)

data.name: WinEvent_DisplayDriver
data.value: Display driver error or driver recovery ({0} {1})

data.name: Risk_FanProblem
data.value: Fan problem: a fan that was spinning this session has stopped while the CPU is hot

data.name: Risk_CrashCount
data.value: Unexpected shutdowns or crashes in 24 h: {0}

data.name: Risk_WinDiskCount
data.value: Disk errors in the Windows log in 24 h: {0}

data.name: Risk_PeakHotspot
data.value: GPU hot spot peaked at {0:0} °C (24 h)

data.name: Risk_RecommendWhea
data.value: Possible hardware problem: check overclocking/XMP profile, memory modules, and drivers.

data.name: Risk_RecommendDisk
data.value: Back up important data and check the drive's health (SMART).

data.name: Risk_RecommendThermal
data.value: Check cooling: fan operation, dust, and case airflow.

data.name: Risk_RecommendSystem
data.value: The computer has crashed or shut down unexpectedly — monitor temperatures and check the power supply.

data.name: Risk_RecommendGpuDriver
data.value: Update the graphics driver with a clean installation.

data.name: Report_Intro
data.value: This report was produced by the Hardware Monitor application. It describes the computer's condition in plain language: first an overall assessment, then metric values with explanations, and finally a glossary of terms.

data.name: Report_LevelsExplanation
data.value: What the levels mean: Good = all values are at normal levels. Warning = a value has exceeded its warning limit or there have been events requiring attention. Critical = something needs action right away.

data.name: Report_VerdictCritical
data.value: CRITICAL: the critical limit has been exceeded

data.name: Report_VerdictWarning
data.value: WARNING: the warning limit has been exceeded

data.name: Report_NoHistoryPeriod
data.value: No history has accumulated for this period yet.

data.name: Report_PeakNear
data.value: came close to the warning limit ({0:0} {1})

data.name: Report_PeakWellBelow
data.value: stayed well below the warning limit ({0:0} {1})

data.name: Report_TypicalIntro
data.value: The computer has typically operated at these levels:

data.name: Report_NoEvents
data.value: No warnings or critical events — a good sign.

data.name: Report_InfoOmitted
data.value: (Routine informational entries, such as application startups, are omitted.)

data.name: Report_Glossary
data.value: - Warning limit and critical limit: temperature and usage limits set in the application. Exceeding the warning limit deserves attention; exceeding the critical limit requires action. - GPU hot spot: the hottest single measurement point on the graphics card — always higher than the general GPU temperature, and that is fine. - WHEA: a hardware error recorded by Windows (e.g. memory, PCIe, or the processor). A single corrected error will not crash the computer, but recurring errors suggest a hardware or overclocking problem. - Kernel-Power 41: a Windows record indicating the computer previously shut down unexpectedly (a crash or power loss). - BSOD / BugCheck: the "blue screen", i.e. a Windows crash. - TDR / display driver error: the graphics driver stopped responding briefly and Windows recovered it. Harmless when occasional; if it recurs, update the driver. - RPM: fan revolutions per minute.

data.name: Insights_Intro
data.value: Updated: {0:d.M.yyyy 'at' HH.mm}. Generated automatically from sensor history (30 days). This file is intended both for the user and for an AI assistant (Claude) to understand and optimize this machine.

data.name: Insights_AiIntroBody
data.value: This file is a summary of this computer's health, generated by the Hardware Monitor application. The data comes from the LibreHardwareMonitor library: readings are taken every second, aggregated into compact history rows and kept for 30 days. In the tables, the average describes the typical level, the peak is the highest momentary reading and the warning limit is the application's alert threshold — a momentary peak near the limit does not necessarily indicate a problem if the average is low. Use this file as context when the user asks about this machine's temperatures, performance, cooling or troubleshooting.

data.name: Insights_TrendsNotEnough
data.value: - Not enough data from the last 7 days for comparison yet.

data.name: Insights_NotEnoughData
data.value: Not enough data yet — history accumulates while the application is running.

data.name: Insights_DisksTableHeader
data.value: | Drive | Avg temp | Peak | Warning limit |

data.name: Insights_CpuHot
data.value: CPU peak temperature {0:0} °C has reached the warning limit ({1:0} °C) — check cooling and dust, consider adjusting fan curves.

data.name: Insights_HotspotHot
data.value: GPU hot spot has reached {0:0} °C (warning limit {1:0} °C) — check case airflow and GPU fans.

data.name: Insights_RamHigh
data.value: RAM usage has reached {0:0} % (warning limit {1:0} %) — memory runs low at times.

data.name: Insights_DiskHot
data.value: Drive {0} peak temperature {1:0} °C exceeds the warning limit ({2:0} °C) — consider an M.2 heatsink or improving case airflow for long writes.

data.name: Insights_Whea
data.value: WHEA hardware errors ({0} in 30 days) — suggests a possible hardware or overclocking problem: check XMP profile, voltages, and drivers.

data.name: Insights_Crashes
data.value: Unexpected shutdowns or crashes ({0} in 30 days) — monitor temperatures under load and check the power supply.

data.name: Insights_WinDisk
data.value: Windows disk errors ({0} in 30 days) — back up your data and check the drive's SMART health.

data.name: Insights_AllGood
data.value: No significant problems detected — values are at normal levels.
